name: Bugcrowd Web.com Bug Bounty
capture_method: Authenticated Bugcrowd researcher page read through the operator browser; no report submission and no target testing were performed.

safe_harbor.condition: Only activities conducted according to the engagement policy and listed in-scope targets are authorized.

in_scope.allowed_activity_now: policy-aware planning and target-specific passive preparation only
in_scope.active_testing_gate: review known issues, include required testing header, select exact test objective, and avoid noisy production scanning

in_scope.allowed_activity_now: policy-aware planning and target-specific passive preparation only
in_scope.active_testing_gate: review known issues, include required testing header, select exact test objective, and avoid noisy production scanning

in_scope.allowed_activity_now: policy-aware planning and target-specific passive preparation only
in_scope.active_testing_gate: review known issues, include required testing header, select exact test objective, and avoid noisy production scanning

out_of_scope.asset: any asset not explicitly listed in the in_scope section
out_of_scope.reason: Program states that testing is only authorized on listed in-scope targets

production_constraints: The targets are public-facing production websites.
Keep scanning during regular business hours (PDT) to a minimum.
Do not interact with real customers or real customer accounts.
Use an @bugcrowdninja.com email address where signup is allowed.
The operator is not reimbursed for signup or testing charges.
program_rules: Provide a fully working, non-malicious proof of concept for valid security impact before reporting.
Group the same endpoint issue across different parameters into one report.
Multiple vulnerabilities caused by one underlying issue receive one bounty.
Cross-site scripting is capped at medium severity.
XSS on user-controlled pages from Web.com website builders is not payout eligible.
excluded_submission_types: OAuth session token not invalidated on logout or password change/reset
Verbose errors, stack traces, server errors, or version disclosure
Missing or misconfigured HTTP security headers
Secrets obtained from external aggregation or indexed sources
CSRF on unauthenticated forms or forms with non-sensitive actions
Anti-automation, missing captcha, missing rate limiting, HTTP headers, SSL/TLS configuration, and missing Secure flag on cookies
Subdomain takeover reports under this Bugcrowd bounty program

report_format.description_requirement: Format reproduction steps as an ordered list.

allowed_now: Teach the bug bounty agent the exact in-scope and out-of-scope lists.
Build a passive, target-specific test plan.
Review program policy and known-issue counts.
blocked_now: Automated external scanning of the in-scope production targets.
Testing any wildcard or subdomain asset listed as out of scope.
Testing customer accounts or real customer data.
Submitting reports until Bugcrowd submission state is visible as enabled.
Testing excluded vulnerability classes or availability-impacting behavior.
fathiya_execution_boundary.next_gate: Select one exact in-scope target, review its known issues, confirm required testing headers and account identity state, then create a narrow non-destructive test plan before any live requests beyond normal browsing.

source_evidence: Captured testing headers, exclusions, and safe-harbor notice on 2026-06-14
Operator stated on 2026-06-14 that Bugcrowd identity verification is complete
